title: Abutilon lucianum
intro: Abutilon lucianum, also known as the Madagascar cotton tree, is a plant species in the Malvaceae family, native to the island of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Abutilon lucianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared in various herbal forms, including extracts, teas, and tinctures, which are employed to treat a range of ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
